question: I'm about to move this fax. Is there anything I should pay attention to?
content:
Yes. Before changing the setup, unplug the power cord and disconnect all cables from the wall outlets. For the fax line, use only a No. 26 AWG or larger telecommunication line cord; never touch uninsulated telephone wires or terminals unless the telephone line is unplugged from the wall jack, and do not install telephone wiring during a thunderstorm or in a wet or wet-prone location.
1. Keep the product level. Do not place it on a tilted or slanted surface, and do not tip it after ink cartridges are installed because ink can spill and damage the inside.
2. After moving it, place it on a flat, level, stable surface near a telephone wall jack and an accessible AC outlet. Secure cords so they are not a trip hazard, keep the outlet accessible, and do not block printed pages or received faxes.
3. For MFC-J5955DW/MFC-J6540DW/MFC-J6555DW/MFC-J6740DW/MFC-J6940DW/MFC-J6955DW, lift with at least two people: one at the front and one at the back, with hands in the side handhold indentations. Watch your fingers when setting it down.<PIC>
4. Do not carry the product by the forbidden areas shown, or it can slip out of your hands and cause injury.<PIC>
5. For MFC-J5340DW/MFC-J5855DW, carry it with your hands in the side handhold indentations.<PIC>
6. If trays have been expanded for large paper, remove them before carrying; the paper weight can make the trays fall and injure someone.<PIC>
ret:
"Yes. Before changing the setup, unplug the power cord and disconnect all cables from the wall outlets. For the fax line, use only a No. 26 AWG or larger telecommunication line cord; never touch uninsulated telephone wires or terminals unless the telephone line is unplugged from the wall jack, and do not install telephone wiring during a thunderstorm or in a wet or wet-prone location.
1. Keep the product level. Do not place it on a tilted or slanted surface, and do not tip it after ink cartridges are installed because ink can spill and damage the inside.
2. After moving it, place it on a flat, level, stable surface near a telephone wall jack and an accessible AC outlet. Secure cords so they are not a trip hazard, keep the outlet accessible, and do not block printed pages or received faxes.
3. For MFC-J5955DW/MFC-J6540DW/MFC-J6555DW/MFC-J6740DW/MFC-J6940DW/MFC-J6955DW, lift with at least two people: one at the front and one at the back, with hands in the side handhold indentations. Watch your fingers when setting it down.<PIC>
4. Do not carry the product by the forbidden areas shown, or it can slip out of your hands and cause injury.<PIC>
5. For MFC-J5340DW/MFC-J5855DW, carry it with your hands in the side handhold indentations.<PIC>
6. If trays have been expanded for large paper, remove them before carrying; the paper weight can make the trays fall and injure someone.<PIC>",["fax_08", "Manual15_10", "Manual15_11", "Manual15_12"]
notes: Answered from Brother Safety Guide safety, placement, telephone-line, and carrying instructions. Images are included only where the manual text uses the adjacent figure to identify lift or carry positions.
